req.title: Cheque deposit by mobile (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image; cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct testable concerns: (1) normal deposit flow for amounts at or below $5,000 (EP positive), (2) MICR decode failure returning 422 (NEG), (3) boundary at $5,000/$5,001 triggering manual-verification decisioning (BVA), (4) idempotency — duplicate Idempotency-Key returns original result (STC), and (5) decision table covering the auto-approve vs manual-review split (DT). Five tests are generated to cover all heuristics. Each test is self-contained, uses fixtures/helpers as placeholders, and stays within the 40-line code limit.

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_below_threshold_returns_pending
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A valid cheque image with decodable MICR line is available
Deposit amount is $1,000 (well within the $5,000 auto-approve partition)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image, amount=1000, unique Idempotency-Key
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains decisioning field indicating auto_approved or queued
trace: R-FIN-094: amounts <= $5,000 do not require manual verification
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_below_threshold_returns_pending(auth_session, valid_cheque_image):
    idempotency_key = <generate_uuid>()
    payload = {
        "amount": 1000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_session.token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert "deposit_id" in body
    assert body.get("decisioning") in ("auto_approved", "queued")

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_invalid_micr_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
An image with an unreadable/corrupt MICR line is available
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with corrupt MICR image, amount=500, unique Idempotency-Key
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error code MICR_DECODE_FAILURE or equivalent
trace: R-FIN-094: error pathway — 422 when image fails MICR decode

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_at_5000_boundary_auto_approved
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A valid cheque image with decodable MICR line is available
Deposit amount is exactly $5,000 (upper boundary of auto-approve partition)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image, amount=5000, unique Idempotency-Key
Capture HTTP response status, body, and decisioning field
expected: decisioning field does NOT indicate manual_review (at boundary, auto-approve applies)
trace: R-FIN-094: cheques above $5,000 require manual verification — $5,000 is the inclusive boundary
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_at_5000_boundary_auto_approved(auth_session, valid_cheque_image):
    idempotency_key = <generate_uuid>()
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_session.token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert "deposit_id" in body
    assert body.get("decisioning") != "manual_review", \
        "$5,000 should not trigger manual review; only amounts strictly above $5,000 should"

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_above_5001_requires_manual_review
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A valid cheque image with decodable MICR line is available
Deposit amount is $5,001 (first value strictly above the $5,000 threshold)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image, amount=5001, unique Idempotency-Key
Capture HTTP response status, body, and decisioning field
trace: R-FIN-094: cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_above_5001_requires_manual_review(auth_session, valid_cheque_image):
    idempotency_key = <generate_uuid>()
    payload = {
        "amount": 5001,
        "currency": "USD",
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_session.token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert "deposit_id" in body
    assert body.get("decisioning") == "manual_review", \
        "Amounts above $5,000 must be queued for manual verification"

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A valid cheque image with decodable MICR line is available
A unique Idempotency-Key is prepared for reuse across two requests
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image, amount=2000, Idempotency-Key=KEY-001 (first submission)
Record deposit_id and status from first response
POST /deposits/mobile-cheque again with same payload and Idempotency-Key=KEY-001 (duplicate submission)
expected: First POST returns HTTP 202 with status=pending and a deposit_id
Second POST returns HTTP 200 or 202 (idempotent replay)
Second response deposit_id equals first response deposit_id
Second response status equals first response status
No duplicate deposit record is created
trace: R-FIN-094: operation must be idempotent — duplicate Idempotency-Key returns original result
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original(auth_session, valid_cheque_image):
    idempotency_key = <generate_uuid>()
    payload = {
        "amount": 2000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "image": valid_cheque_image,
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_session.token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }
    first = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 202
    first_body = first.json()
    assert "deposit_id" in first_body

    second = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code in (200, 202)
    second_body = second.json()
    assert second_body["deposit_id"] == first_body["deposit_id"], \
        "Idempotent replay must return the original deposit_id"
    assert second_body["status"] == first_body["status"], \
        "Idempotent replay must return the original status"
